title: Greg Brockman Defends $30B OpenAI Stake: ‘Blood, Sweat, and Tears’
description: OpenAI’s cofounder and president revealed in federal court on Monday that he’s one of the largest individual stakeholders in the AI lab.

title: OpenAI’s cozy partner Cerebras is on track for a blockbuster IPO
description: AI chip maker Cerebras is heading for a blockbuster IPO that could value it at $26.6 billion or more. Its relationship with OpenAI is deep and rich.

description: During a high-volume deluge test on the new Orbital Launch Pad 2 one of the methalox gas generators that supplies high-pressure nitrogen for the deluge system exploded. description: Stuart Russell is a long-time AI researcher who thinks governments need to restrain frontier labs.

title: Elon Musk sent ominous texts to Greg Brockman, Sam Altman after asking for a settlement, OpenAI claims
description: Musk texted OpenAI's president and co-founder saying that he and CEO Sam Altman "will be the most hated men in America" if OpenAI doesn't settle the suit.

title: Anthropic and OpenAI are both launching joint ventures for enterprise AI services

title: Ouster releases REV8 OS sensor family with native-color lidar
description:
Ouster says its Rev8 OS sensors are the first to have native color and include the OS1 Max with double the range and resolution of Rev7.
The post Ouster releases REV8 OS sensor family with native-color lidar appeared first on The Robot Report.

title: The Humanoid Robot Market Is Smaller Than It Looks
description: Humanoid robot narratives usually begin with market size, not with the physics of the work, and that leads to distorted expectations. title: EU Green Hydrogen Scheme Embraces High-Tech Solar Foods
description:
The Finnish startup Solar Foods leverages a natural microorganism, renewable energy, and green hydrogen to produce Solein, a protein-rich food supplement produced through gas fermentation.
The post EU Green Hydrogen Scheme Embraces High-Tech Solar Foods appeared first on CleanTechnica.

title: SpaceX Starship Flight 12 May 12
